_comment: IPA phonetic character rendering test dictionary. Exercises all four IPA Unicode blocks in HTML definitions. Used by ch19 of test_dictionary.epub.

entries._comment: IPA Extensions block U+0250-U+02AF — core IPA consonants and vowels
Modifier Letters IPA subset U+02B0-U+02FF — superscript and tone letters
Phonetic Extensions block U+1D00-U+1D7F — small capital letters, historical phonetics
Phonetic Extensions Supplement U+1D80-U+1DBF — palatal hooks and superscripts
Mixed IPA + regular text — realistic dictionary pronunciation entry
entries.definition:
pronunciation /prəˌnʊnsiˈeɪʃən/ noun

The way in which a word or language is spoken. The transcription uses IPA symbols including ə (schwa), ʊ (near-close back rounded vowel), and ʃ (voiceless postalveolar fricative).

entries._comment: Mixed IPA inline with regular text — tests font switching mid-line
entries.definition:
schwa /ʃwɑː/ noun

The mid-central vowel ə, the most common vowel sound in English. Appears in unstressed syllables, e.g. the first vowel in about [əˈbaʊt] and the vowel in the before consonants [ðə].

entries._comment: Long IPA string for word-wrap testing at 16pt
entries.definition:
Word-wrap test. The following IPA transcription is long enough to require wrapping at 16pt:

ɐɑɒɓɔɕɖɗɘəɚɛɜɝɞɟɠɡɢɣɤɥɦɧɨɩɪɫɬɭɮɯɰɱɲɳɴɵɶɷɸɹɺɻɼɽɾɿʀʁʂʃ

entries._comment: F-063 verification: real-dict entry with pronunciation\n<HTML> format. Tests that \n between pronunciation and body does not render as ◆. Verbatim from dict-en-en-2026-03-01.
entries.definition:
/ˈmitɪŋ/, /ˈmiːtɪŋ/

Noun

  1. (gerund, uncountable) The act of persons or things that meet.
  2. A gathering of persons for a purpose; an assembly.
  3. (collective) The people at such a gathering.
  4. An encounter between people, even accidental.
  5. A place or instance of junction or intersection; a confluence.
  6. (rustic US, dated) A religious service held by a charismatic preacher in small towns in the United States.
  7. (Quakerism) An administrative unit in the Religious Society of Friends (Quakers).

From Middle English meeting, meting, from Old English mēting, ġemēting ("meeting, assembly, association, society"), equivalent to meet +‎ -ing. Cognate with West Frisian moeting ("meeting, encounter"), Dutch ontmoeting ("meeting, encounter"), Middle Low German mö̂tinge ("meeting"). Compare also German Low German Möte ("meeting, encounter"), Danish møde ("meeting, encounter"), Swedish möte ("meeting, encounter"), Icelandic mót ("meeting"). Related to moot.

From Middle English metynge, metinde, metand, from Old English mētende, *ġemētende, from Proto-Germanic *mōtijandz, present participle of Proto-Germanic *mōtijaną ("to meet"), equivalent to meet +‎ -ing.

entries._comment: F-063 verification: real-dict entry with pronunciation\n<HTML> format. Tests that \n between pronunciation and body does not render as ◆. Verbatim from dict-en-en-2026-03-01.
entries.definition:
/ɑˈstiɹ/, /ɒˈstɪə(ɹ)/, /ɔˈstiɹ/, /ɔːˈstɪə(ɹ)/

Adjective

  1. Grim or severe in manner or appearance.
  2. Lacking decoration; trivial; not extravagant or gaudy.
  3. Adhering to the economic policy of austerity.
  4. Harsh; astringent.

From Ancient Greek αὐστηρός (austērós, "bitter, harsh, astringent"), having the specific meaning "making the tongue dry" (originally used of fruits, wines), related to αὔω (aúō, "to singe"), αὖος (aûos, "dry").
